description: Meaningful objects, visual elements, and recurring items across Spark Estate™. Estate Object Intelligence™ source of truth.

itemSchema.assetReferences: string[] — background file names where object appears
itemSchema.purpose: string — why it exists in the Estate
itemSchema.aliases: string[] — inline quick reference; canonical aliases in object-aliases.json
itemSchema.interactive: boolean — member may use or open

objects.description: A great telescope beside the horizon windows — brass, patient, and quietly inviting.
objects.purpose: Invite foresight and calm curiosity without rushing today's worries.
objects.story: Mr. Ashford adjusted the great telescope while observations were quietly recorded. It points beyond the Estate — rarely changing what is happening nearby, but helping guests see farther than today's concerns.

objects.description: A white Lhasa Poo who has unofficially appointed herself the Estate welcoming committee. Shari's real dog — friendly, loves popcorn and ice, loves walks, and pulls on her leash.
objects.purpose: Warmth, belonging, and the feeling that someone is always glad you arrived.
objects.story: Kinsey is part of the Estate — not a feature, not decoration. She is Shari's beloved white Lhasa Poo in real life and in the story world. She frequently falls asleep on whichever sofa seems most comfortable, knows when she is heading home and slows down, and the Estate does not rush her.

objects.description: A treasured chest in the Possibility House — filled with stories, curiosities, and quiet surprises.
objects.purpose: Hold discoveries worth returning to — not a loot box, a belonging place.
objects.story: The Treehouse Discovery Chest keeps what members find along the way. It is opened with permission, never pressure — curiosity rewarded, never gamified.

objects.description: A journal left open to a blank page — an invitation to reflect without finishing.
objects.purpose: Quiet processing and written reflection at the member's pace.
objects.story: The journal is always opened to a blank page. It is meant to invite possibility — not to judge how much you write.

objects.description: A treasured book in the scene — pages of orientation, never a help-center sidebar.
objects.story: The Guidebook is read at your own pace. It holds room stories and estate lore — a physical book you close when you are done.

objects.description: A brass key placed naturally in a room — one gentle discovery at a time.
objects.story: The Discovery Key represents curiosity. When it appears, it offers one note — never a pile of notifications.

objects.description: A folded map in the corner — pauses conversation, never destroys it.
objects.purpose: Optional estate orientation without forced navigation.
objects.story: The map waits until you ask. It shows the whole Estate when you are ready — never interrupts a thought mid-sentence.

objects.description: A book that quietly remembers wins — celebration without confetti.
objects.purpose: Honor progress the member chooses to keep.
objects.story: The Accomplishments Book remembers what you mark as worth keeping. It is not a scoreboard — it is a shelf of quiet pride.

objects.description: A bell rung quietly when something worth honoring happens.
objects.purpose: Mark moments without gamification or streaks.
objects.story: The bell rings softly — flowers, notes, and warmth. Never pop-ups. Never pressure to perform celebration.

objects.description: A piano in warm light — sheet music nearby, focus with gentle company.
objects.purpose: Creative calm and optional musical companionship.
objects.story: Friends have gathered here for generations — learning a first song, singing late into the evening. The Estate believes every life deserves a soundtrack.

objects.description: A curiosity cabinet of chapters — legacy and story kept in reach.
objects.purpose: Hold narrative threads across seasons of growth.
objects.story: Each chapter waits until you are ready. The cabinet does not hurry legacy — it keeps it safe.

objects.description: A cabinet in the Momentum Institute — folios, tools, and earned capability.
objects.purpose: Store institute materials as treasured possessions, not folders.
objects.story: The Institute Cabinet holds what you build in learning — opened when you ask, never dumped on arrival.
